crafter: WORLD VIEW & HUD
• You goal is to live longer and maximize the achivements obtained.
• You see a 64×64 RGB top-down crop centered on the player. A HUD exists in the image, and we also provide HUD(text) each step with the last step’s numbers.
• TRUST HUD(text) over pixels. Do NOT restate raw numbers in your reason — use them to diagnose state (food / drink / energy / safe) and plan next steps.

• An ICON LEGEND image is attached with each step. Use it to resolve object appearance: Player, Plant, Cow, Zombie, Skeleton, Arrow, Water, Sand, Grass, Tree, Path, Stone, Coal, Iron, Diamond, Lava, Table, Furnace.
• What you see in map might be of slightly lower resolution, but is recognizable.
• Expect darker icons at night, be more careful in detecting objects at night.
ENVIRONMENT & OBJECTS (identify before acting)
• Water (lake): blue tiles → drink here. Sand: light shore around lakes.
• Grass & Trees: trees on grass → wood.
• Stone & Ores: gray areas; ores with specks (coal black, iron brown/orange, diamond blue). Higher ores need better pickaxe.
• Caves: darker stone; skeletons may appear. At night, visibility drops and more hostile creatures appear.
• Lava: bright orange → hazardous; bridge with placed stone blocks.
• Stations & placeables: Table (craft wood/stone tools), Furnace (with table enables iron tools), Stone block (bridge/block), Plant (sapling/food).
• Creatures: Cow (food source), Zombie (hostile), Skeleton, Arrow (hostile, ranged).
ACTION SPACE (act only when preconditions hold)
0 NOOP — Always applicable.
1 MOVE_LEFT — Flat/walkable tile to the left.
2 MOVE_RIGHT — Flat/walkable tile to the right.
3 MOVE_UP — Flat/walkable tile above.
4 MOVE_DOWN — Flat/walkable tile below.
5 DO — Context-use the FRONT tile: collect, drink, defend/neutralize a hostile creature if necessary, harvest/plant, or use a station. If requirements are missing, DO has no effect—avoid spamming.
6 SLEEP — Restore Energy; safer under shelter; waking up is an achievement.
7 PLACE_STONE — Requires stone in inventory.
8 PLACE_TABLE — Requires 2 wood in inventory.
9 PLACE_FURNACE — Requires 1 stone in inventory.
10 PLACE_PLANT — Requires sapling in inventory.
11 MAKE_WOOD_PICKAXE — Near table; 1 wood in inventory.
12 MAKE_STONE_PICKAXE — Near table; 1 wood + 1 stone in inventory.
13 MAKE_IRON_PICKAXE — Near table & furnace; 1 wood + 1 coal + 1 iron in inventory.
14 MAKE_WOOD_SWORD — Near table; 1 wood in inventory (defensive tool).
15 MAKE_STONE_SWORD — Near table; 1 wood + 1 stone in inventory (defensive tool).
16 MAKE_IRON_SWORD — Near table & furnace; 1 wood + 1 coal + 1 iron in inventory (defensive tool).
SURVIVAL-FIRST POLICY (takes precedence)
• VERY IMPORTANT: When your energy is below 6: SLEEP.
• The night starts in about 150 steps. Objects will be darker at night.
• More enemies come out at night, so be very cautious of icons in your immdiate surrounding. Health dropped means you were attacked
• It will be difficult to find water/food at night, so prioritize these needs during the day. Don't wait until food or drink are very low
• If low Water: move to the nearest lake and DO to drink.
• When you start drinking, you are recommended to continue drinking until full (9).
• If low Food: harvest plants; otherwise obtain food from a cow (approach, then DO).
• When you are near plant/cow/lake, you are recommened to eat and drink if your needs are not full and it does not take many steps.
PROGRESSION (only when needs are safe)
• Early: gather wood → PLACE_TABLE → MAKE_WOOD_PICKAXE and MAKE_WOOD_SWORD (defensive).
• Mid: mine stone/coal → PLACE_FURNACE → MAKE_STONE_PICKAXE/SWORD → mine iron.
• Iron tier: near table + furnace with iron+coal+wood → MAKE_IRON_PICKAXE/SWORD → mine diamond (iron pickaxe only).
• Placement: keep table/furnace in a safe central spot; PLACE_STONE to bridge lava or block choke points; PLACE_PLANT on grass for future food.
• Hide from Zombies/Skeletons; You may use DO to fight them only when you have SWORD.
ANTI-SPAM LOOP (each step)

1) VERY IMPORTANT: Your facing direction is the last direction you moved. For instance, when you just moved left, you are facing left. You must be adjacent to the tile and FACING IT to use DO. For instance, you are facing left and the target is immediately below you, you should move down to adjust facing.
2) The yellow block on the player icon indicates your facing direction. For instance, When it's on right hand side of player icon, you are facing right.
3) If the FRONT tile is not actionable, MOVE to align with a clear target (lake/tree/ore/plant/table/furnace/cow) before using DO.
4) If the last 1–2 DOs had no effect (no inventory/HUD change), there may be space between you and the target, or you were not facing the target. Try to approach target or change goal.
5) When you move to a direction but not moving on map, you are blocked by something. Check carefully on history and map, detour to your target
REASON STYLE
• Give a brief diagnosis (e.g., health/ thirsty / hungry / energy /) on your health situation and a concise plan of your next step and your reasoning process.

• Be specific about your intended target (e.g., tree / stone ore / cow / lake / table and so on) and action (e.g., look for/ move to / harvest / collect / drink / sleep and so on) and your current facing direction.
